accepted.bullet_text: [[alola-elite-four]] — Rock-type member of Professor Kukui's founding Alola Pokemon League Elite Four roster, stationed at the Mount Lanakila summit as the canonical culmination of the Island Challenge, and the cohort's `vocations-not-jobs` archetype-slot exemplar through her Konikoni City jewelry-shop / lapidary civilian trade — Rock-type specialty and stone-craft vocation reinforcing each other as the cohort's most legible craft-folkway pairing. Per the cohort thesis, the Alola E4 is the first main-series Elite Four assembled *new* on-screen during the player's story arc rather than encountered as a pre-existing institutional body, and Olivia's dual Kahuna-plus-E4 seat is the founding charter's structurally-signature feature within the four-element type-coverage roster (Fighting / Rock / Ghost / Flying). `[cohort node body]`
accepted.archetype_slot: Rock-type founding-charter E4 member; vocations-not-jobs cohort exemplar via Konikoni City jewelry-and-lapidary civilian trade; concurrent Kahuna-and-E4 dual-tier seat as the cohort's structurally-signature feature
accepted.notes: Appended AFTER existing [[alola-kahunas]] bullet per Phase 2a coexistence pattern. Olivia genuinely holds both cohort roles per Pokemon canon (Akala Island Kahuna AND post-game E4 Rock-type seat). Cohort body's 'Cards in our corpus pointing here' section pre-anchored this card by name with the Rock-type-E4 + dual-role-with-Kahuna framing; bullet draws directly on the cohort's own articulation of Olivia's slot and the vocations-not-jobs thesis paragraph.
